intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant Ananassa porteana. However, I can provide you with information on Ananas comosus (Pineapple) and Ananas ananassoides (Paya), and their related species. 

Ananas comosus (Pineapple) is a tropical plant with edible fruits and is a member of the Ananas genus. The health benefits of Pineapple include anti-inflammatory properties due to the presence of bromelain, an enzyme that helps in reducing swelling and pain. The bioactive constituents of Pineapple include bromelain, vitamin C, manganese, and various polyphenolic compounds. The most common herbal preparations made with Pineapple include juice, tea, and topical creams for pain relief and skin care.

If you meant Ananas ananassoides (Paya), it's a plant species with edible fruits and is a close relative of Ananas comosus (Pineapple). The health benefits of Paya include antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties due to the presence of various bioactive compounds. The bioactive constituents of Paya include bromelain, vitamin C, and various polyphenolic compounds. The most common herbal preparations made with Paya include juice, tea, and topical creams for pain relief and skin care.

taxonomy: Ananassa porteana is a synonym of the species Ananas comosus. This means it is classified as a type of pineapple (Ananas comosus) under the family Bromeliaceae. The classification of Ananassa porteana is further grouped into the kingdom Plantae. It is native to South America and is widely cultivated for its edible fruit. Ananassa porteana is often used interchangeably with Ananas comosus in botanical contexts.
properties: Ananassa porteana, also known as the wild guava, has been studied for its potential therapeutic properties. The plant's leaves and fruits are rich in antioxidants and have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The ant-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of Ananassa porteana may help alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and other infections. Some studies suggest that extracts from the plant may have anti-cancer and anti-diabetic properties, although more research is needed to confirm these findings. Further investigation is required to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Ananassa porteana.
constituents: Ananassa porteana, also known as the Ananas bracteatus or the Prickly Pineapple, is a species of pineapple native to South America.
